takeaways.title: One episode can become a real benchmark contract
takeaways.claim: The public sample is converted into 5,821 frames, 1,161 aligned 20-frame windows, and an 8,378-dimensional feature contract.
takeaways.boundary: This is a task-development benchmark, not cross-episode generalization.

takeaways.title: Chronological splits expose action-class shift
takeaways.claim: Earlier all-feature action classifiers reach high macro-F1 on their local split, but the 12-task chronological action/subtask heads are much harder because later held-out windows include unseen labels.
takeaways.boundary: This is an important leakage/split lesson, not evidence that action recognition is solved.

takeaways.title: Small neural heads help dynamic and temporal probes
takeaways.claim: The MLP heads substantially improve hand trajectory forecasting, temporal-order verification, and motion/visual synchronization.
takeaways.boundary: These gains are within one episode and should be re-tested on held-out episodes.

takeaways.title: Retrieval and reconstruction remain the harder multimodal problems
takeaways.claim: Ridge/cosine retrieval remains stronger than the neural projection on this sample, and cross-modal reconstruction still has negative R2.
takeaways.boundary: The current reconstruction task is feature-vector reconstruction, not depth, mesh, NeRF, or Gaussian splatting.

takeaways.title: The next scientific unit is held-out episodes, not more adjacent windows
takeaways.claim: The prepared Qwen3-Omni path targets 32 episodes from 32 sessions, but it remains data-gated until access and held-out evaluation complete.
takeaways.boundary: No real 32-episode fine-tune is claimed until gated data is available locally and held-out evaluation runs.
